topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp autumn air did little to settle the knot tightening in Maya’s stomach. She kept glancing toward her desk, a furtive dart of her eyes catching the edge of her open journal. It was a nervous habit, a constant check, as if the paper itself held some secret judgment. When she finally dared to look directly at Liam, her cheeks felt hot, a flush creeping up her neck. He was engrossed in a thick paperback, a book Maya had explicitly asked him to return hours ago. A strange, cold dread washed over her as she realized the book wasn't just open; it was open to a page filled with her own looping handwriting, the ink dark and undeniably hers. Her hands began to tremble, and she quickly pulled her gaze away, focusing intensely on the dust motes dancing in the afternoon sun slanting through the window.

[story 2]

I felt a prickle of alarm crawl up my spine every time I entered our shared room. Everything felt amplified—the squeak of the floorboard, the distant hum of the refrigerator. Today, the source of the unease was undeniable. I found my sketchbook slightly askew, and when I picked it up, the pages were filled with my latest anxieties, my rambling thoughts about the upcoming presentation, all neatly transcribed in my handwriting. My breath hitched. I watched Liam across the room, his posture relaxed, seemingly oblivious. I stood there, frozen, my fingers gripping the sketchbook so tightly my knuckles turned white. A desperate need to retreat flooded me; I mumbled something about needing water and fled to the bathroom, the sound of my own hurried footsteps echoing loudly in the silence.

[story 3]

The silence in the dorm room was oppressive, thick enough to choke on. I kept catching myself staring at the corner of my desk, a place I usually kept clear. Today, however, it was disturbed. I crept over, my movements slow and deliberate, like I was approaching a startled animal. There, lying face-up, was my diary. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat against the quiet. I could feel the blood draining from my face. When I finally looked up, Liam was sitting at his own side of the room, seemingly reading something else, but the way he was looking—a slight, almost imperceptible smirk—sent a wave of mortification through me. I quickly dropped down onto my bed, pulling my knees to my chest, trying to shrink into the mattress, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole.
